mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against a zero-mean null.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms synchronized demand and supply growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns turn negative when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally on inflation prints above 0.4 percent month-over-month, or the negative correlation between CPI surprises and 5-day returns vanishes in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals sustained consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue expansion, which directly supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict 20-day forward returns, or the relationship reverses during periods of wage-price spirals.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 1.1 million units indicates severe credit tightening and weakening consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST drops below 1.1 million units annualized
failure_condition: Housing starts below the threshold consistently coincide with equity market rallies, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52 percent in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while payroll growth decelerates indicates productivity gains and margin expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The productivity divergence signal yields negative forward returns, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1 percent real GDP growth confirms economic stagnation that erodes corporate earnings forecasts, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.0 percent
failure_condition: Equity markets remain flat or positive when GDP growth drops below 1 percent, or the signal loses predictive power after adjusting for monetary policy expectations.
